issue_title: [Bug] MinerEvaluationCache.store runs before duplicate-GitHub penalty, persisting pre-penalty scores; future API failure restores them as-if-never-penalized
issue_body:
## Description:
In `neurons/validator.py:store_or_use_cached_evaluation`, the cache **store** path runs in the same loop as the cache **restore** path — and that combined call happens **before** `detect_and_penalize_miners_sharing_github` in `gittensor/validator/oss_contributions/reward.py:get_rewards`. As a result, a miner whose eval is about to be zeroed for sharing a GitHub account is **first cached with the pre-penalty data**, then penalized.

[#600](https://github.com/entrius/gittensor/pull/600) made sure the **current round's** zeroed score reaches the DB by removing penalized UIDs from `cached_uids` so the storage-skip-set excludes them. But the cache content itself was not touched — the cached entry still holds pre-penalty PR buckets with full `earned_score`, `pioneer_dividend`, etc.

When a future round hits a transient GitHub fetch failure for that UID **and the duplicate partner has since deregistered or rotated GitHub**, the cache restore returns the round-T pre-penalty snapshot, the post-restore `detect_and_penalize` no longer trips (no duplicate now), and the validator silently uses the resurrected pre-penalty scores — undoing the lingering effect of the round-T penalty under conditions the operator can't see.

## Source

[`gittensor/validator/oss_contributions/reward.py:148-160`](https://github.com/entrius/gittensor/blob/test/gittensor/validator/oss_contributions/reward.py#L148-L160):

```python
# If evaluation of miner was successful, store to cache, if api failure, fallback to previous successful evaluation if any
cached_uids = self.store_or_use_cached_evaluation(miner_evaluations)   # ← cache.store runs HERE

# Adjust scores for duplicate accounts; returns penalized UIDs so they are
# removed from cached_uids and their zeroed scores are written to DB.
penalized_uids = detect_and_penalize_miners_sharing_github(miner_evaluations)   # ← penalty runs AFTER
cached_uids -= penalized_uids
```

[`neurons/validator.py:160-165`](https://github.com/entrius/gittensor/blob/test/neurons/validator.py#L160-L165) — the inner store call:

```python
if not miner_eval.github_pr_fetch_failed:
    if miner_eval.total_prs > 0:
        self.evaluation_cache.store(miner_eval)   # ← `failed_reason` is None here even for soon-to-be-penalized duplicates
    continue
```

[`gittensor/classes.py:store`](https://github.com/entrius/gittensor/blob/test/gittensor/classes.py) — gate is on `failed_reason`:

```python
def store(self, evaluation: 'MinerEvaluation') -> None:
    if evaluation.failed_reason is not None:
        return
    ...
    cached_eval = self._build_cache_entry(evaluation)   # shallow copy: failed_reason is None at this snapshot
    self._cache[evaluation.uid] = CachedEvaluation(...)
```

`_build_cache_entry` does `copy.copy(evaluation)`, which captures `failed_reason=None` at copy time. The subsequent penalty mutates the **live** eval but the **cached** copy retains `failed_reason=None` and the full pre-penalty PR buckets.

## Concrete scenario

1. **Round T.** UID-100 (legitimate) and UID-250 (attacker) both broadcast PATs for the same GitHub account. Both produce normal-looking PR buckets (same GitHub → same scoring data).
2. `store_or_use_cached_evaluation(...)` runs — both UIDs are cached with pre-penalty PR buckets and `failed_reason=None`.
3. `detect_and_penalize_miners_sharing_github(...)` zeroes both. DB rows for round T correctly show zero.
4. **Round T+1.** UID-250 deregisters or rotates to a different GitHub. UID-100 is now alone with that GitHub. UID-100's GitHub fetch **fails** (transient `/user` outage, mirror outage, network blip).
5. Cache fallback: `cached_eval = self.evaluation_cache.get(100, ...)` returns the round-T pre-penalty snapshot — `failed_reason=None`, full `merged_pull_requests`, `mirror_merged_prs`, full `earned_score`, `pioneer_dividend`.
6. `miner_evaluations[100] = cached_eval`. UID-100 is now in `cached_uids`.
7. `detect_and_penalize_miners_sharing_github(...)` runs on the restored eval. UID-100's `failed_reason` is `None` (cached), `github_id` is the shared GH from round T. **No other UID has this GH now** (UID-250 is gone), so no duplicate detected, no penalty.
8. `finalize_miner_scores` runs on UID-100's restored eval — full pre-penalty scores survive. Storage path persists them (UID-100 is in `cached_uids` so DB write is skipped, but the eval's normalized reward in `get_rewards`'s return value is high).

The lingering effect of the round-T penalty is silently erased the first time UID-100 hits an API failure with the partner gone. Operators have no signal — the cache doesn't expose "this was penalized when stored," so the resurrection passes every defensive gate.

## Why this matters

- **Penalty escape vector.** A miner who shares a GitHub once and gets penalized can wait for any of `(transient GitHub /user outage | mirror outage | partial-page failure)` to combine with the partner being gone, and the cached pre-penalty scores quietly resurface.
- **Asymmetry with the merged [#600](https://github.com/entrius/gittensor/pull/600) intent.** That fix made the **current round** correct (zero score reaches DB). The **future round** is not addressed; the bad data is still in cache.
- **Hard to detect.** The resurrection looks identical to a normal cache fallback in logs.

## Suggested fix

Either of:

1. **Re-store after penalty.** After `detect_and_penalize_miners_sharing_github` runs, walk `penalized_uids` and call `self.evaluation_cache._cache.pop(uid, None)` so future cache lookups for those UIDs don't return the pre-penalty snapshot. Re-storing the post-penalty (zeroed) eval is a no-op because `evaluation_cache.store` skips entries with `failed_reason is not None`.

2. **Reorder.** Move the cache **store** (but not the cache **restore**) to after the penalty step. The restore must run first because penalty needs the restored eval to detect persistent duplicates; the store can move after.

Option 1 is the smaller change — adds one helper (`evaluation_cache.evict(uid)`) and one loop in `get_rewards`.

## Distinct from prior filings

- [#596](https://github.com/entrius/gittensor/issues/596) (closed) and [#600](https://github.com/entrius/gittensor/pull/600) (merged) addressed the **current-round DB-write skip**. They did not touch the cache content. This issue is about **future-round cache resurrection** of pre-penalty data.
- [#869](https://github.com/entrius/gittensor/issues/869) / [#1006](https://github.com/entrius/gittensor/issues/1006) cover `_zero_for_duplicate_penalty` field-list completeness — a separate concern (penalty content vs cache timing).
